name: Abdullah ibn Ali
title: First Sacrifice of Abbas
shortDescription: The first son of Umm al-Banin (SA) who entered the battlefield at Abbas's command to prove his loyalty to the Imam.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib was the full brother of Hazrat Abbas (AS) and another son of Lady Umm al-Banin (SA). He was a 25-year-old youth whose name was chosen by his father Ali (AS). He was raised in the lap of a mother who from day one had taught her children that Hussain (AS) is their Imam and Master, not just a brother.

On the afternoon of Ashura, after the martyrdom of the companions, Hazrat Abbas (AS), who was the standard-bearer and commander, turned to his brothers and said: 'May my soul be sacrificed for you, go forth and defend your Master'. Abdullah was the first among the brothers to answer this call and volunteered to go to the battlefield.

He entered the field with a tall stature and an iron spirit, swinging his sword and reciting this epic poetry: 'I am the son of Ali, the possessor of generosity and bounty... and the defender of my brother Hussain, who is a shining sun'. He charged into the heart of the enemy and began a heavy battle.

With great skill, Abdullah brought down dozens of Umar Sa'd's soldiers, once again showcasing the bravery of Bani Hashem to the enemies. Finally, Hani ibn Thubayt al-Hadrami targeted him with an arrow or sword strike, martyring him.

Abdullah's martyrdom as the first martyr among the full brothers of Hazrat Abbas (AS) paved the way for his other brothers (Uthman and Ja'far) to sacrifice their lives one after another before the eyes of Karbala's standard-bearer, displaying the peak of loyalty.
quoteArabic: أَنَا ابْنُ ذِي النَّجْدَةِ وَالأَفْضالِ، ذاكَ عَلِيٌّ الخَيِّرُ الفَعّالِ
quote: I am the son of a brave and generous man; he is Ali (AS) who was a doer of good.
